name: Ali ibn Al-Hussain
title: Ali Akbar
shortDescription: The eldest son of Imam Hussain, who was the most similar person to the Prophet of Islam in appearance, morals, and speech.
fullStory:
Ali ibn Al-Hussain (AS), known as 'Ali Akbar', was the eldest son of Imam Hussain (AS) and Layla bint Abi Murrah. He was a handsome and elegant youth, whose physical features and moral virtues were so strikingly similar to the Prophet of Islam (PBUH) that whenever the companions and the household longed to see the Prophet, they would gaze upon his captivating face. This unparalleled resemblance gave him a very special place in the heart of his father and the rest of Bani Hashem.

During the Ashura uprising, he was one of the main pillars of the caravan and always marched forward with deep insight and full awareness of the path to martyrdom. On the journey toward Karbala, at a resting place called 'Qasr Bani Muqatil', Imam Hussain (AS) briefly fell asleep on his horse. Upon waking, he uttered the phrase of returning to God (Inna lillahi wa inna ilayhi raji'un). Ali Akbar immediately asked the reason, and when the Imam explained that he saw in a dream a voice saying this caravan is moving toward death, Ali Akbar asked with exemplary decisiveness and bravery: 'Dear father! Are we not on the right path?' Upon hearing a positive answer, he declared: 'Then when we are on the right path, we have no fear of death in the way of truth'.

According to the documented and precise report of the book Al-Irshad written by Sheikh Mufid, on the day of Ashura, the first person from the family of Abu Talib (Bani Hashem) to enter the battlefield for combat and be killed was Ali Akbar (AS). A handsome and fearless youth, after obtaining permission from his father, he charged into the field while Imam Hussain (AS) sent him off with tearful eyes. With crushing battle cries, he demonstrated the nobility and righteousness of his path to the enemy.

Sheikh Mufid writes in Al-Irshad: Ali Akbar (AS) struck the heart of the enemy army with unparalleled bravery and fought heroically. He repeatedly attacked the enemy ranks and inflicted heavy casualties upon them, but severe thirst and numerous wounds had sapped his strength. Meanwhile, Murrah ibn Munqidh al-Abdi, who harbored a grudge against him and had sworn to leave his father grieving, cowardly struck him with a spear and brought him down.

According to the continuation of the report in Al-Irshad, as Ali Akbar fell to the ground, the vengeful enemies surrounded him from all sides and cut his pure body to pieces with sword blows. Imam Hussain (AS), with boundless haste and sorrow, rushed to the blood-soaked body of his son. Weeping bitterly, he said: 'May God kill the people who killed you, O my son... How audacious this nation has become against God and in violating the sanctity of the Messenger of God... After you, dust be upon this world'.
quoteArabic: يا أبتِ ألسنا على الحق؟ إذن لا نبالي أوقعنا على الموت أو وقع الموت علينا.
quote: Dear father! Are we not on the right path? Then we have no fear of death in the way of truth.
